extra.reasoning: The property requires NOT(rec_drug_a AND rec_drug_b). rec_drug_a is set true only by r2 (priority 50), whose antecedent is elderly AND low_ejection_fraction AND qt_prolongation AND immunosuppressed AND pregnancy. rec_drug_b is set true only by r3 (priority 50), whose antecedent is elderly. No rule ever sets rec_drug_a or rec_drug_b to false, so priority/conflict resolution is irrelevant per output (each output has only one writing rule). Whenever r2's antecedent holds, elderly must be true, so r3 also fires. The counterexample sets elderly, low_ejection_fraction, qt_prolongation, immunosuppressed, and pregnancy all true (other inputs false). Then r2 fires -> rec_drug_a = true, and r3 fires (elderly true) -> rec_drug_b = true. Both are jointly true, violating mutual exclusion.
